info.title: Pod Engine API
info.description: Pod Engine podcast intelligence API. Search 4M+ podcasts, retrieve metadata, transcripts, charts, and more. See https://www.podengine.ai/api-docs for the full interactive documentation.

bearerAuth.description: Pod Engine API key. Get one at https://www.podengine.ai/get-started.

post.description: Analyze the relevancy of a podcast to a project using AI

cursor.description: Pagination cursor for fetching the next page of results. This cursor is stateless and does not expire. However, because it uses search_after pagination, results may be inconsistent if the underlying data changes between requests (e.g., you may see duplicate or missing results if documents are added/removed).

pageSize.description: Number of results to return per page

project.description: Details about any Project this search is associated with. If included will filter out podcasts related to the project.

excludeIgnoredPodcasts.description: If true, will not include any podcasts marked as ignored on this project in the results.

excludePodcastsOnList.description: If true, will not include any podcasts on the project list in the results.

includePodcastRelevancy.description: If the podcast relevancy should be included with the search results. Note that including this will limit your search results to 20 total and also slow down the search results response

excludeRelvancyValues.description: If provided, will exclude podcasts with these relevancy values. Valid values are 1-5. Note this is a server side filter and will only work if includePodcastRelevancy is set.

forceRecalculateRelevancy.description: If true, will force a recalculation of the podcast relevancy values for this search. Note this will slow down the search results response and should only be used if you believe the relevancy values are out of date.

projectId.description: [DEPRECATED] Project ID to associate the search with

searchTerms.description: Terms to search for in podcast title and description

phraseMatch.description: Whether to match the search term as a phrase

slop.description: How many words apart tokens can appear in a document to be considered a match

excludeItunesGenres.description: iTunes genres to exclude from results

excludePodcastIds.description: Specific podcast IDs to exclude from results

excludeEpisodeIds.description: Specific episode IDs to exclude from results

foundedSince.description: Filter podcasts founded after this date

hasPrimaryContact.description: Filter podcasts that have primary contact information

hasSocials.description: Filter podcasts with specific social media presence from beehiiv, behance, bluesky, discord, facebook, github, instagram, linkedin, linktree, mastodon, medium, patreon, pinterest, reddit, snapchat, soundcloud, substack, threads, tiktok, tumblr, twitch, twitter, vimeo, whatsapp, youtube

includeItunesGenres.description: iTunes genres to include in results

includePodcastIds.description: Specific podcast IDs to include in results

lastEpisodeDateSince.description: Filter podcasts with episodes published after this date

podcastCountries.description: Filter podcasts by country of origin using ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes, for example us

podcastAuthorityScore.description: Filter podcasts by their Authority Score

sortOrder.description: Specify how results should be sorted

get.description: Get a historical chart

schema.description: The type of chart, one of apple, spotify

The chart category defaulting to 'top podcasts'. Apple podcasts supports the following: "top podcasts", "arts", "business", "comedy", "education", "fiction", "government", "health & fitness", "history", "kids & family", "leisure", "music", "news", "religion & spirituality", "science", "society & culture", "sports", "technology", "true crime", "tv & film"
The country of the chart in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format, for example "us". Defaults to us.
The number of chart positions to return. If not provided, all positions are returned
The date of the chart in YYYY-MM-DD format. If null will return the latest chart

chartType.description: The type of chart, one of apple, spotify

category.description: The chart category defaulting to 'top podcasts'. Apple podcasts supports the following: "top podcasts", "arts", "business", "comedy", "education", "fiction", "government", "health & fitness", "history", "kids & family", "leisure", "music", "news", "religion & spirituality", "science", "society & culture", "sports", "technology", "true crime", "tv & film"

country.description: The country of the chart in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format, for example "us". Defaults to us.

positionsLimit.description: The number of chart positions to return. If not provided, all positions are returned

date.description: The date of the chart in YYYY-MM-DD format. If null will return the latest chart

chartType.description: The type of chart, one of apple, spotify

category.description: The chart category defaulting to 'top podcasts'. Apple podcasts supports the following: "top podcasts", "arts", "business", "comedy", "education", "fiction", "government", "health & fitness", "history", "kids & family", "leisure", "music", "news", "religion & spirituality", "science", "society & culture", "sports", "technology", "true crime", "tv & film"

country.description: The country of the chart in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format, for example "us". Defaults to us.

chartDate.description: The date of the chart in YYYY-MM-DD format

authorityScore.description: This is the weighted total authority score of the podcast, out of 100

qualityScore.description: This is the quality score of the podcast, out of 100

youtubeScore.description: This is the YouTube score of the podcast, out of 100

socialScore.description: This is the social score of the podcast, out of 100

engagementScore.description: This is the engagement score of the podcast, out of 100

hasVideo.description: Whether the podcast RSS feed declares a directly-downloadable video file for this episode. This does not include YouTube videos or Apple Podcasts HLS streams.

videoUrl.description: A direct, downloadable URL to the episode video file when hasVideo is true; otherwise null. HLS playlists and streaming-only sources are excluded.

ageRange.description: The age range of the audience as a string.

genderSkew.description: How the audience is skewed in terms of gender.

educationLevel.description: The probable education levels of the audience.

incomeLevel.description: The probable income levels of the audience.

exampleListenerProfile.description: An example profile of the audience. This is a description of the audience that you can use to describe the audience to an advertiser.

post.description: Create a new guest profile with basic information. The profile is created in ACTIVE status along with an associated project. Required fields are firstName, lastName, and shortBio. Optional fields include contact details, social links, and bio details. When provided, LinkedIn URLs trigger a profile scrape, website URLs trigger a website crawl, and company names trigger a SERP search — all run asynchronously in the background. A guest appearances search is also triggered automatically.

shortBio.description: A short bio for the guest

longBio.description: A longer bio for the guest

topicsToDiscuss.description: Topics the guest wants to discuss

additionalContext.description: Any additional context about the guest

createAlert.description: Optionally create a person alert for this guest, defaults to true

email.description: The email address associated with the podcast contact

anyOf.description: The timestamp when the email was last verified, or null if it has not been verified

syntaxValid.description: Whether the email address has valid syntax (e.g. correctly formatted local and domain parts), or null if not yet checked

mxValid.description: Whether the email's domain has valid MX records indicating it can receive mail, or null if not yet checked

anyOf.description: The timestamp when the email was last deep-verified for deliverability, or null if not run

deliverabilityStatus.description: Deep deliverability status: valid, invalid, catch-all, unknown, spamtrap, abuse, or do_not_mail

deliverabilityReason.description: Sub-classification providing more detail on the deliverability result

isFreeEmailProvider.description: Whether the address belongs to a known free-email provider (e.g. gmail, yahoo)

suggestedCorrection.description: Suggested correction for likely typos, e.g. gmial.com -> gmail.com

engagementScore.description: This is the engagement score of the podcast, out of 100

chartType.description: The type of chart, one of apple, spotify

category.description: The chart category defaulting to 'top podcasts'. Apple podcasts supports the following: "top podcasts", "arts", "business", "comedy", "education", "fiction", "government", "health & fitness", "history", "kids & family", "leisure", "music", "news", "religion & spirituality", "science", "society & culture", "sports", "technology", "true crime", "tv & film"

country.description: The country of the chart in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format, for example "us". Defaults to us.

positionsLimit.description: The number of chart positions to return. If not provided, all positions are returned

limit.description: The number of charts to return

chartDate.description: The date of the chart in YYYY-MM-DD format

chartType.description: The type of chart, one of apple, spotify

country.description: The country of the chart in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format, for example "us". Defaults to us.

get.description: Refresh podcast metadata and thumbnail from RSS feed. Rate limited to once per minute per podcast.

schema.description: Podengine ID, Apple ID, or slug

socialScore.description: This is the social score of the podcast, out of 100

engagementScore.description: This is the engagement score of the podcast, out of 100

affiliateOrganizationName.description: The name of the affiliate organization

affiliationType.description: The type of affiliation from the list: academic, branded, celebrity, government, independent, legacy media, podcast company, new media, not for profit, religious, other, unknown

ageRange.description: The age range of the audience as a string.

genderSkew.description: How the audience is skewed in terms of gender.

educationLevel.description: The probable education levels of the audience.

incomeLevel.description: The probable income levels of the audience.

exampleListenerProfile.description: An example profile of the audience. This is a description of the audience that you can use to describe the audience to an advertiser.

get.description: Poll for updates across your team. Returns a chronological feed of events (completed requests, new guest appearances, podcasts added to projects, alert matches) since a given timestamp or cursor. Designed for regular polling — use the returned cursor for efficient subsequent calls.

schema.description: ISO 8601 timestamp. Return events after this point. Ignored if cursor is provided. Max lookback: 30 days.
Opaque cursor from a previous response. Takes precedence over since.
Comma-separated list of event types to include: request_completed, guest_appearance, listed_podcast_added, alert_match. Default: all.
Filter listed_podcast_added events to a specific project.
Filter guest_appearance events to a specific guest profile.
Filter alert_match events to a specific alert configuration.
Max events per page (1-100, default 50).

since.description: The resolved since timestamp used for this query.

cursor.description: The cursor provided in this request, if any.

eventTypes.description: The event types included in this query.

limit.description: The maximum number of events per page.

guestProfileId.description: Guest profile filter applied, if any.

alertConfigId.description: Alert config filter applied, if any.

options.description: The resolved options used for this query.

cursor.description: Pass this value as the cursor query parameter on your next poll. Null if no results.

hasMore.description: True if there are more events beyond this page.

post.description: Search for podcasts by title, description, or RSS feed URL

cursor.description: Pagination cursor for fetching the next page of results. This cursor is stateless and does not expire. However, because it uses search_after pagination, results may be inconsistent if the underlying data changes between requests (e.g., you may see duplicate or missing results if documents are added/removed).

pageSize.description: Number of results to return per page

project.description: Details about any Project this search is associated with. If included will filter out podcasts related to the project.

excludeIgnoredPodcasts.description: If true, will not include any podcasts marked as ignored on this project in the results.

excludePodcastsOnList.description: If true, will not include any podcasts on the project list in the results.

includePodcastRelevancy.description: If the podcast relevancy should be included with the search results. Note that including this will limit your search results to 20 total and also slow down the search results response

excludeRelvancyValues.description: If provided, will exclude podcasts with these relevancy values. Valid values are 1-5. Note this is a server side filter and will only work if includePodcastRelevancy is set.

forceRecalculateRelevancy.description: If true, will force a recalculation of the podcast relevancy values for this search. Note this will slow down the search results response and should only be used if you believe the relevancy values are out of date.

projectId.description: [DEPRECATED] Project ID to associate the search with

searchTerms.description: Terms to search for in podcast title and description

phraseMatch.description: Whether to match the search term as a phrase

slop.description: How many words apart tokens can appear in a document to be considered a match

excludeItunesGenres.description: iTunes genres to exclude from results

excludePodcastIds.description: Specific podcast IDs to exclude from results

excludeEpisodeIds.description: Specific episode IDs to exclude from results

foundedSince.description: Filter podcasts founded after this date

hasPrimaryContact.description: Filter podcasts that have primary contact information

hasSocials.description: Filter podcasts with specific social media presence from beehiiv, behance, bluesky, discord, facebook, github, instagram, linkedin, linktree, mastodon, medium, patreon, pinterest, reddit, snapchat, soundcloud, substack, threads, tiktok, tumblr, twitch, twitter, vimeo, whatsapp, youtube

includeItunesGenres.description: iTunes genres to include in results

includePodcastIds.description: Specific podcast IDs to include in results

lastEpisodeDateSince.description: Filter podcasts with episodes published after this date

podcastCountries.description: Filter podcasts by country of origin using ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes, for example us

podcastAuthorityScore.description: Filter podcasts by their Authority Score

sortOrder.description: Specify how results should be sorted

cursor.description: Pagination cursor for fetching the next page of results. This cursor is stateless and does not expire. However, because it uses search_after pagination, results may be inconsistent if the underlying data changes between requests (e.g., you may see duplicate or missing results if documents are added/removed).

projectId.description: [DEPRECATED] Project ID to associate the search with

phraseMatch.description: Whether to match the search term as a phrase

slop.description: How many words apart tokens can appear in a document to be considered a match

excludeItunesGenres.description: iTunes genres to exclude from results

excludePodcastIds.description: Specific podcast IDs to exclude from results

excludeEpisodeIds.description: Specific episode IDs to exclude from results

includeItunesGenres.description: iTunes genres to include in results

includePodcastIds.description: Specific podcast IDs to include in results

lastEpisodeDateSince.description: Filter podcasts with episodes published after this date
